title: XV
html:
XV

Had this volume been a farce, which, unless everyoneвҖҷs life and opinions are to be looked upon as a farce as well as mine, I see no reason to supposeп»ҝвҖ”the last chapter, Sir, had finished the first act of it, and then this chapter must have set off thus.

Ptr..r..r..ingп»ҝвҖ”twingп»ҝвҖ”twangп»ҝвҖ”prutп»ҝвҖ”trutп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”вҖҷtis a cursed bad fiddle.п»ҝвҖ”Do you know whether my fiddleвҖҷs in tune or no?п»ҝвҖ”trut..prut..п»ҝвҖ”They should be fifths.п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”вҖҷTis wickedly strungп»ҝвҖ”trп»ҝвҖҠп»ҝвҖҰ a.e.i.o.u.-twang.п»ҝвҖ”The bridge is a mile too high, and the sound post absolutely down,п»ҝвҖ”elseп»ҝвҖ”trut .В . prutп»ҝвҖ”hark! вҖҷtis not so bad a tone.п»ҝвҖ”Diddle diddle, diddle diddle, diddle diddle, dum. There is nothing in playing before good judges,п»ҝвҖ”but thereвҖҷs a man thereп»ҝвҖ”noп»ҝвҖ”not him with the bundle under his armп»ҝвҖ”the grave man in black.п»ҝвҖ”вҖҷSdeath! not the gentleman with the sword on.п»ҝвҖ”Sir, I had rather play a Caprichio to Calliope herself, than draw my bow across my fiddle before that very man; and yet IвҖҷll stake my Cremona to a JewвҖҷs trump, which is the greatest musical odds that ever were laid, that I will this moment stop three hundred and fifty leagues out of tune upon my fiddle, without punishing one single nerve that belongs to himп»ҝвҖ”Twaddle diddle, tweddle diddle,п»ҝвҖ”twiddle diddle,п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝtwoddle diddle,п»ҝвҖ”twuddle diddle,п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝprut trutп»ҝвҖ”krishп»ҝвҖ”krashп»ҝвҖ”krush.п»ҝвҖ”IвҖҷve undone you, Sir,п»ҝвҖ”but you see heвҖҷs no worse,п»ҝвҖ”and was Apollo to take his fiddle after me, he can make him no better.

Diddle diddle, diddle diddle, diddle diddleп»ҝвҖ”humп»ҝвҖ”dumп»ҝвҖ”drum.

вҖ”Your worships and your reverences love musicп»ҝвҖ”and God has made you all with good earsп»ҝвҖ”and some of you play delightfully yourselvesп»ҝвҖ”trut-prut,п»ҝвҖ”prut-trut.

O! there isп»ҝвҖ”whom I could sit and hear whole days,п»ҝвҖ”whose talents lie in making what he fiddles to be felt,п»ҝвҖ”who inspires me with his joys and hopes, and puts the most hidden springs of my heart into motion.п»ҝвҖ”If you would borrow five guineas of me, Sir,п»ҝвҖ”which is generally ten guineas more than I have to spareп»ҝвҖ”or you Messrs. Apothecary and Taylor, want your bills paying,п»ҝвҖ”thatвҖҷs your time.
